channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: GameStop tables $55.5 billion takeover bid for eBay
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 5, 2026

2 Min Read

U.S. video game retailer GameStop has made a $55.5 billion offer to purchase e-commerce juggernaut, eBay.

The offer values eBay at $125.00 per share, comprising 50 percent cash and 50 percent GameStop common stock. 

GameStop said its offer values eBay at a 46 percent premium on the company's unaffected closing price on February 4, 2026—the day GameStop began accumulating its stake in the company.

GameStop said it will leverage $20 billion in debt financing from TD Securities to facilitate the transaction. The retailer has already acquired a 5 percent economic stake in eBay. 

The non-binding proposal already has the support of the GameStop board.

In a note to investors, eBay confirmed receipt of the offer and said it will now "carefully review and consider" the proposal to determine whether it is in the best interests of shareholders. 

GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen said he will lead the combined company if the takeover moves ahead, and explained he will be compensated solely based on performance. "I will receive no salary, no cash bonuses, and no golden parachute," he added in a letter to eBay chairman of the board, Paul Pressler.

Related:Wizards of the Coast declines to voluntarily recognize game worker union

Cohen claims eBay is spending more on sales and marketing without actually attracting users. As such, he said GameStop would deliver $2 billion of annualised cost reductions within 12 months of closing the transaction. He also claims GameStop's numerous brick-and-mortar locations in the United States will bolster eBay's business. 

"We will take $2 billion of annualized costs out within twelve months of close: $1.2 billion from Sales & Marketing, $300 million from Product Development, and $500 million from General & Administrative," said Cohen. 

"[...] On cost reductions alone, eBay’s diluted GAAP earnings per share from continuing operations would increase from $4.26 to $7.79 in year one. GameStop’s ~1,600 US locations give eBay a national network for authentication, intake, fulfillment, and live commerce."

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/gamestop-tables-55-5-billion-takeover-bid-for-ebay
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Wizards of the Coast declines to voluntarily recognize game worker union
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 5, 2026

2 Min Read

Magic: The Gathering Arena (MTG Arena) developers who are attempting to unionize as United Wizards of the Coast—CWA (UWOTC—CWA) claim Wizards of the Coast and Hasbro have declined to voluntarily recognize the union. 

UWOTC—CWA asked leadership at Wizards of the Coast and Hasbro to voluntarily recognize the union by May 1, 2026. That deadline has now passed, and union workers claim their request was met with silence. 

"In our letter to management this past Monday, we called on Wizards of the Coast and Hasbro to take the high road and voluntarily recognize our union by Friday, May 1st. That deadline has now come and gone, with no direct response from the company," reads a union statement. "Management claims to be committed to ensuring every person is heard, valued, and supported. However, when we spoke up about our desire to unionize, they responded only to the press."

Last week, a Wizards of the Coast spokesperson told Kotaku the company was carefully reviewing the union's request. 

Related:Meet the indie studios funding other indie studios

"Our employees are the lifeblood of what makes us great, and we are committed to fostering a workplace where every person feels heard, valued, and supported," they said. 

"We believe we have a strong connection with everyone at Wizards of the Coast and that direct relationship with our employees is essential to how we work together to capture the imagination of our fans and players, inspiring a lifetime love of our games. We appreciate hearing about the needs and interests of our employees through this filing, and will respond through the appropriate process."

UWOTC—CWA previously filed an election petition with the National Labor Relations Board to push for recognition in the event Wizards of the Coast and Hasbro failed to meet their demands. The group said it is attempting to unionize to "increase staff wellbeing" by pushing for generative AI guardrails and layoff protections.

More than 30,000 people have signed a public petition calling on Wizards of the Coast to recognize UWOTC—CWA. Union members are now urging fans to "keep up the pressure" on their employer. 

"We still maintain that the success of our union is inevitable. The company has witnessed the tremendous outpouring of support and positive attention that our campaign has received from the public and the press," added UWOTC—CWA. 

"They have received our voluntary recognition letter with the names of a super-majority of the Arena team. We are confident that we are going to win recognition of this union; it's only a question of time. We look forward now towards the NLRB election, which we expect to be held within the coming weeks."

Related:Report: VR specialist Survios has laid off the majority of its development team

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/wizards-of-the-coast-declines-to-voluntarily-recognize-game-worker-union
